title: From/Into
description: The `From` and `Into` traits provide type conversions. Implementing `From` automatically gives you `Into`.
instructions: Implement the From trait to convert from a default value.
hints: Split the string by comma and parse the parts.
Use .split(',') to get name and age parts.
explanation: The `From` trait defines how to create a type from another type. When you implement `From<T> for U`, you automatically get `Into<U> for T`. This is why both `Person::from("...")` and `"...".into()` work. The `From` trait is widely used in Rust for type conversions and is preferred over custom conversion functions.
